title: HVAC Technician
description: An HVAC Technician in India installs, maintains, and repairs heating, ventilation, and air conditioning systems in residential, commercial, and industrial settings. They work with various equipment, including air handlers, chillers, and ductwork, and often troubleshoot issues to ensure optimal system performance. Their daily tasks may involve inspecting systems, performing routine maintenance, and responding to emergency calls for repairs or replacements.
top_govt_colleges: National Institute of Construction Management and Research
top_private_colleges: National Institute of Refrigeration and Air Conditioning
Indian Institute of Refrigeration and Air Conditioning
National Institute of Building Science and Technology
Institute of Refrigeration and Air Conditioning

career_progression.year_0_2: Assist experienced technicians, learn trade skills, and complete apprenticeships.
career_progression.year_3_5: Work independently, take on more complex tasks, and pursue certifications.
career_progression.year_6_10: Supervise teams, manage projects, and consider starting own business.
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Start own HVAC contracting business, offer services to residential and commercial clients.
career_progression.global_pathway: Opportunities exist in the Middle East, Southeast Asia, and the United States for experienced HVAC technicians.

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Starting salary is low, but opportunities for growth and advancement exist.
common_misconceptions: HVAC work is only for men
HVAC requires a high level of education
